source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Add and subtract numbers with up to 4 digits using the formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction where appropriate.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack develops place-value alignment, exchange and checking in columnar addition and subtraction, while retaining estimation and mental-method choice.

objective.child_goal: I can line up place values, exchange when needed and check a written calculation.
objective.statement: Use and explain formal columnar addition and subtraction with numbers up to four digits.
misconceptions: Aligns digits by the edge of the numeral instead of by place value.
Exchanges a ten, hundred or thousand but does not record or use the exchanged value correctly.
Treats subtraction as subtracting the smaller digit from the larger digit in each column.
Uses a written method automatically even when estimation or a mental method would be clearer.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The value a digit has because of its position.
vocabulary.audio_script: Place value tells us whether a digit means ones, tens, hundreds or thousands.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A labelled thousands, hundreds, tens and ones chart.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Rename one place-value unit as ten of the next smaller unit.
vocabulary.audio_script: Exchange one ten for ten ones, or one hundred for ten tens, without changing the total.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One place-value counter opening into ten counters in the next column.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A close answer used to predict or check a calculation.
vocabulary.audio_script: An estimate is a sensible nearby answer that helps us notice mistakes.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Rounded numbers feeding a check gauge.

vocabulary.child_explanation: An operation that undoes another operation.
vocabulary.audio_script: Addition and subtraction are inverse operations because each can undo the other.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two arrows travelling in opposite directions between the same totals.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Round each number, predict the size of the answer, then place every digit in its correct column.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate magnitude and place-value knowledge before procedural work.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Estimate first, then line up thousands, hundreds, tens and ones.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two numbers move into a labelled place-value chart while a check gauge shows the estimated range.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: A written method keeps each place-value calculation organised so no value is lost.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Frame column methods as recorded place-value reasoning rather than a memorised digit routine.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Each column has a value. We calculate from the ones and record every exchange.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Place-value counters sit above matching written digits in four columns.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: For 2,786 + 1,457, add the ones first. Thirteen ones become 3 ones and 1 exchanged ten. Continue one column at a time.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model addition with consecutive exchanges and connect every written mark to quantity.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ten ones are exchanged for one ten. The total stays the same as the counters move to a new column.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Counters exchange beside the calculation; the small exchanged digit appears only after the counters regroup.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build the calculation, choose whether to exchange, and enter one column result at a time.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Fade from concrete counters to an independent written layout while preserving place-value language.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Say the value of the column, make any exchange, then record the digit.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A split view pairs draggable counters with keyboard-editable calculation cells.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: A learner says 4,032 - 1,758 = 3,326. Find the first column where the method stops representing the calculation.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect larger-from-smaller digit subtraction and insecure exchange across a zero.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Do not swap the digits. Rename a larger place-value unit and keep the total equivalent.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The incorrect calculation can be inspected column by column against place-value counters.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach your companion how an estimate and the inverse can check 3,641 - 1,286.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather explanation evidence and connect calculation accuracy to independent checking.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Explain what answer size you expect, then show how addition can undo the subtraction.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: An estimate gauge and inverse number sentence appear beside the completed calculation.

manipulatives.purpose: Connect place-value counters, exchanges and formal written recording for addition and subtraction.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners drag or keyboard-move counters between labelled columns, trigger an exchange only when ten units or a required subtraction rename is present, and type the matching written digit.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Every column is a labelled grid region; Tab moves by column, arrow keys change counters, E performs an available exchange, and a live region announces totals before and after exchange. Colour is never the sole place-value cue, audio can be replayed, and a no-drag mode exposes equivalent stepper buttons.

manipulatives.purpose: Support estimation, first-error identification and inverse checking without requiring handwriting.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect one column at a time, mark the first invalid step and choose or enter a repair.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: The strip has a linear screen-reader order, visible focus, 44-pixel targets, optional dyslexia-friendly font, and a reveal-one-column mode that reduces visual load.

practice.interleaving_notes: Alternate addition and subtraction only after alignment is secure; interleave estimation and mental-method choices so the formal algorithm is used where appropriate rather than by default.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Secure alignment of numbers with different digit lengths before calculation.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two addends or minuend/subtrahend up to four digits + labelled place-value grid + one tempting edge-aligned layout
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include zeros as placeholders and mixed three-/four-digit pairs; never imply that commas are calculation columns.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Practise no, single and consecutive exchanges in four-digit addition.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: four-digit addition + counter model state + editable column cells + estimate band
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Balance exchange locations and include calculations whose totals remain within an age-appropriate range.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Practise subtraction with one or more exchanges, including exchange across a zero.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: four-digit subtraction + place-value rename path + column response + inverse check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Require a mathematically valid rename sequence and keep language consistent: exchange or rename, not borrow.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify the first incorrect step, explain its place-value effect and repair the calculation.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: worked calculation with one seeded error + first-error selection + repair choice + explanation stem
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Seed only one conceptual error per item; distractors must reflect known misconceptions rather than arbitrary arithmetic slips.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose between mental and written methods, estimate first and verify with the inverse.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: calculation pair + context-free method choice + estimate interval + inverse verification
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Accept more than one efficient strategy where justified; do not reward speed over a valid explanation.

body.prompt: Place 3,406 and 782 into the grid ready for addition.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Start by placing both ones digits in the ones column.
The 7 in 782 means 7 hundreds.
question_variants.explanation: Digits are aligned by place value, so 6 and 2 are in the ones column and 4 and 7 are in the hundreds column.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "3", "4", "0", "6", "", "7", "8", "2". Digits are aligned by place value, so 6 and 2 are in the ones column and 4 and 7 are in the hundreds column.
feedback.repair: Start by placing both ones digits in the ones column. The 7 in 782 means 7 hundreds. Use that clue to repair edge alignment, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this place value layout, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "3", "4", "0", "6", "", "7", "8", "2": Digits are aligned by place value, so 6 and 2 are in the ones column and 4 and 7 are in the hundreds column.
feedback.misconception_check: edge alignment
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Calculate 2,786 + 1,457. Record every exchange.

hints: 6 ones plus 7 ones makes 13 ones.
Record 3 ones and exchange 1 ten.
question_variants.explanation: The exchanges preserve the total: 13 ones becomes 1 ten and 3 ones, then the tens and hundreds also require an exchange, giving 4,243.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 4243; exchange digits: 1, 1, 1. The exchanges preserve the total: 13 ones becomes 1 ten and 3 ones, then the tens and hundreds also require an exchange, giving 4,243.
feedback.repair: 6 ones plus 7 ones makes 13 ones. Record 3 ones and exchange 1 ten. Use that clue to repair exchange not recorded, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this column calculate, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. body.prompt: Calculate 4,032 - 1,758 and use addition to check.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Exchange through the tens column so there are enough ones.
After calculating, add the difference and the subtrahend.
question_variants.explanation: Renaming one hundred as ten tens and then one ten as ten ones makes the subtraction possible. The difference is 2,274, and 2,274 + 1,758 = 4,032.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 2274; inverse check: "2274 + 1758 = 4032". Renaming one hundred as ten tens and then one ten as ten ones makes the subtraction possible. The difference is 2,274, and 2,274 + 1,758 = 4,032.
feedback.repair: Exchange through the tens column so there are enough ones. After calculating, add the difference and the subtrahend. Use that clue to repair larger minus smaller digit, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this column calculate, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 2274; inverse check: "2274 + 1758 = 4032": Renaming one hundred as ten tens and then one ten as ten ones makes the subtraction possible. The difference is 2,274, and 2,274 + 1,758 = 4,032.
feedback.misconception_check: larger minus smaller digit
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Alignment bay 1: place 3,406 and 782 for addition before calculating.
body.narration_script: Place 3,406 and 782 into thousands, hundreds, tens and ones.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move the learner's named counter or record an indicated digit/explanation without choosing the mathematical step.
body.place_value_chart_route: Labelled thousands, hundreds, tens and ones cells accept digits by tap, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing or adult scribing; commas never occupy a value column.
body.base_ten_exchange_route: Static before/after counters show one larger unit renamed as ten equal smaller units with totals displayed as equal; physical counters are optional.
body.partial_products_grouping_route: Multiplication uses labelled place-value partial products; division uses equal-group trays and an inverse multiplication check, with a linear text table alternative.
body.step_planner_route: ESTIMATE–CHOOSE–MODEL–CALCULATE–CHECK reveals one stage at a time and preserves completed stages.
body.concrete_route: Use optional place-value counters, base-ten equipment, exchange mats or equal-group trays; no learner is required to touch shared materials.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter labelled grid, large digits, generous spacing, pattern plus text rather than colour-only meaning and static before/after exchange frames.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one operation and active column at a time, mask completed inactive columns only on request, and retain every correct digit during repair.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed responses replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: restore one calm calculation station in the Number Workshop
gamification.reward: one blueprint stamp for a represented, estimated or checked idea
gamification.retry_message: Your correct place-value evidence stays. Open one model or check clue and continue without penalty.

body.procedural_meaning_policy: Every written mark must be linked to place value, equal-value exchange, partial products, equal grouping or an inverse check; unexplained digit tricks are not accepted.

hints: Name the value of the active column or quantity before operating on its digit.
Estimate, use a concrete/visual model if useful, then check with magnitude or the inverse.
question_variants.explanation: The grid represents values, not printed edges: ["3","4","0","6"] and [" ","7","8","2"].

feedback.correct: Workshop station restored through align place values. The grid represents values, not printed edges: ["3","4","0","6"] and [" ","7","8","2"].
feedback.repair: Return both ones digits to the labelled ones column, rebuild leftwards and only then calculate.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: The grid represents values, not printed edges: ["3","4","0","6"] and [" ","7","8","2"].
feedback.strategy_message: Mental, written, concrete, visual, pointing, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid when the mathematical reasoning is shown; speed and handwriting are never scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct columns and models, inspect the first uncertain place-value step, then retry without losing progress.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the edge alignment idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.reduced_load_route: Show one operation and active column at a time, mask completed inactive columns only on request, and retain every correct digit during repair.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed responses replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

Estimate, use a concrete/visual model if useful, then check with magnitude or the inverse.
question_variants.explanation: The zero is a placeholder in the tens column; it keeps every other digit at its correct value.

feedback.correct: Workshop station restored through zero placeholder. The zero is a placeholder in the tens column; it keeps every other digit at its correct value.
feedback.repair: Return both ones digits to the labelled ones column, rebuild leftwards and only then calculate.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: The zero is a placeholder in the tens column; it keeps every other digit at its correct value.
feedback.strategy_message: Mental, written, concrete, visual, pointing, AAC and adult-scribed routes are equally valid when the mathematical reasoning is shown; speed and handwriting are never scored.
feedback.retry: Keep correct columns and models, inspect the first uncertain place-value step, then retry without losing progress.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the edge alignment idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.prompt: Value lens 17: what value does the 2 represent in 2,630?
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move the learner's named counter or record an indicated digit/explanation without choosing the mathematical step.
body.place_value_chart_route: Labelled thousands, hundreds, tens and ones cells accept digits by tap, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing or adult scribing; commas never occupy a value column.
body.base_ten_exchange_route: Static before/after counters show one larger unit renamed as ten equal smaller units with totals displayed as equal; physical counters are optional.
body.partial_products_grouping_route: Multiplication uses labelled place-value partial products; division uses equal-group trays and an inverse multiplication check, with a linear text table alternative.
body.step_planner_route: ESTIMATE–CHOOSE–MODEL–CALCULATE–CHECK reveals one stage at a time and preserves completed stages.
body.concrete_route: Use optional place-value counters, base-ten equipment, exchange mats or equal-group trays; no learner is required to touch shared materials.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter labelled grid, large digits, generous spacing, pattern plus text rather than colour-only meaning and static before/after exchange frames.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one operation and active column at a time, mask completed inactive columns only on request, and retain every correct digit during repair.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed responses replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: restore one calm calculation station in the Number Workshop
gamification.reward: one blueprint stamp for a represented, estimated or checked idea

body.place_value_chart_route: Labelled thousands, hundreds, tens and ones cells accept digits by tap, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, pointing or adult scribing; commas never occupy a value column.
body.base_ten_exchange_route: Static before/after counters show one larger unit renamed as ten equal smaller units with totals displayed as equal; physical counters are optional.
body.partial_products_grouping_route: Multiplication uses labelled place-value partial products; division uses equal-group trays and an inverse multiplication check, with a linear text table alternative.
body.step_planner_route: ESTIMATE–CHOOSE–MODEL–CALCULATE–CHECK reveals one stage at a time and preserves completed stages.
body.concrete_route: Use optional place-value counters, base-ten equipment, exchange mats or equal-group trays; no learner is required to touch shared materials.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter labelled grid, large digits, generous spacing, pattern plus text rather than colour-only meaning and static before/after exchange frames.
body.reduced_load_route: Show one operation and active column at a time, mask completed inactive columns only on request, and retain every correct digit during repair.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed responses replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Aligns digits by the edge of the numeral instead of by place value.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: A shorter number is placed against the thousands side or its ones digit is not beneath the other ones digit.
repair_sequence: Read the value of the final digit in each number.
Place both ones digits in the labelled ones column.
Build leftwards and read each aligned column aloud.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner independently aligns a three-digit and four-digit pair and explains one column's value.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Exchanges a ten, hundred or thousand but does not record or use the exchanged value correctly.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Counter total is correct before exchange but the written column result omits or double-counts the exchanged unit.
repair_sequence: Display the total value before exchange.
Exchange one unit for ten equal smaller units and announce that the total is unchanged.
Match the moved counter to one written exchange digit, then recalculate the next column.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner completes a new two-exchange calculation with each exchanged unit recorded exactly once.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats subtraction as subtracting the smaller digit from the larger digit in each column.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: An answer contains positive digit differences but fails the inverse check, especially where the top digit is smaller.
repair_sequence: Name the subtraction direction using the full quantities.
Model a place-value exchange without changing the minuend's total.
Recalculate the column and verify the finished difference with addition.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner exchanges rather than swapping digit order and completes a valid inverse check.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Uses a written method automatically even when estimation or a mental method would be clearer.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects a multi-step layout for a near-multiple or complement calculation and cannot estimate the answer.
repair_sequence: Estimate the result and inspect the number relationship.
Compare one mental strategy with the written method.
Choose a method and justify why it is efficient and reliable.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner selects and justifies an efficient method for a fresh pair while still using a formal method accurately when appropriate.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a plain four-column grid, one static highlight at a time, no celebratory particle effects and a persistent mute control.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving counters with before/after exchange panels; preserve the same values, narration and focus order.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read the whole calculation, then announce the active column and its value; provide replay buttons beside every worked step.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Pair words with symbols, keep instructions to one action per sentence and leave the calculation visible while hints are read.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Reveal only the current column, show a six-step progress strip and pause automatically after each exchange until the learner continues.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, allow unlimited estimate revisions and provide a no-drag stepper interface with undo.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Label incorrect entries as a place-value clue, retain correct columns during repair and praise checking or strategy changes.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach place value, column, exchange, estimate, sum, difference and inverse with counters and dual-coded labels.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires at least 80% accuracy across aligned addition and subtraction, including one exchange, with a reasonable estimate. Secure requires 90% across mixed exchange structures, first-error analysis and independent inverse checking on two retained sessions.
evidence.parent_summary: Can organise four-digit addition and subtraction by place value, record exchanges and check whether an answer is sensible.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Alignment, estimate, exchange path, final accuracy, first-error diagnosis, inverse check and method-choice explanation are stored separately so a correct answer does not hide insecure procedure.
next_actions: Route edge-alignment errors to the place-value grid before further calculation.
Route exchange errors to concrete counters and require an unchanged-total explanation.
Interleave mental strategies and contextual problems once formal recording is secure.
Schedule mixed addition/subtraction retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Deterministic Year 4 written-methods pilot bank with 240 variants: four curated variants remain unchanged and 236 review candidates deepen place-value alignment, meaningful addition exchanges, subtraction renaming, inverse checks, estimates, missing digits, first-error diagnosis, method choice and multistep transfer. Curriculum-aligned short multiplication and division appear only as representation and method-choice links using partial products or equal grouping. Every generated task provides concrete, visual, reduced-load and alternative-input SEND/dyscalculia routes, rich corrective feedback and pressure-free workshop missions. Selected narration uses ElevenLabs references requiring human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent mathematics, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
